domains.name: ELT with Spark SQL and Python

questions.text: Which of the following can be used to simplify and unify siloed data architectures that are specialized for specific use cases?

options.A: None of these
options.B: Data lake
options.C: Data warehouse
options.D: All of these

questions.explanation: The data lakehouse combines the flexibility of a data lake with the structure and governance of a data warehouse, unifying previously siloed architectures.

questions.text: Which of the following data lakehouse features results in improved data quality over a traditional data lake?

options.A: A data lakehouse provides storage solutions for structured and unstructured data.
options.B: A data lakehouse supports ACID-compliant transactions.
options.C: A data lakehouse allows the use of SQL queries to examine data.
options.D: A data lakehouse stores data in open formats.
options.E: A data lakehouse enables machine learning and artificial intelligence workloads.

questions.explanation: ACID transactions are the core feature that improves data quality by ensuring consistency, reliability, and prevention of data corruption — something traditional data lakes lack.

questions.text: Which of the following benefits of using the Databricks Lakehouse Platform is provided by Delta Lake?

options.A: Manipulate data using various languages
options.B: Real-time notebook collaboration
options.C: Alert setup for query failures
options.D: The ability to support batch and streaming workloads

questions.explanation: Delta Lake enables unified batch and streaming workloads on the same table using the same APIs, which is a core Delta Lake benefit.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the storage organization of a Delta table?

options.A: Single file containing all elements
options.B: Data in single file, metadata elsewhere
options.C: Collection of files that contain data, history, metadata, and other attributes
options.D: Collection containing only data

questions.explanation: A Delta table is stored as a collection of Parquet data files plus a _delta_log directory containing JSON and checkpoint files for transaction history and metadata.

questions.text: Which of the following is hosted completely in the control plane of the classic Databricks architecture?

options.A: Worker node
options.B: JDBC data source
options.C: Databricks web application
options.D: Databricks Filesystem

questions.explanation: The Databricks web application (UI), cluster management, job scheduler, and notebook server all reside in the control plane (Databricks-managed cloud account). Compute (driver and worker nodes) and data live in the customer data plane.

questions.text: Which of the following is stored in the Databricks customer's cloud account?

options.A: Databricks web application
options.B: Cluster management metadata
options.C: Repos
options.D: Data

questions.explanation: In the classic architecture, the data plane (customer's cloud account) stores data files and runs compute. The control plane (Databricks account) stores notebooks, repos, and cluster metadata.

questions.text: A data organization leader is upset because data analysis and engineering teams are producing differing reports due to siloed architectures. Which of the following describes a benefit if the organization migrates to a unified Databricks Lakehouse Platform?

options.A: Both teams would autoscale work as data size evolves
options.B: Both teams would use the same source of truth for their work
options.C: Both teams would reorganize to report to the same department
options.D: Both teams would collaborate on projects in real-time
options.E: Both teams would respond more quickly to ad-hoc requests

questions.explanation: A lakehouse eliminates silos by providing a single source of truth so both analytical and engineering teams work from the same data layer.

questions.text: Which of the following describes why a data lakehouse can be used for both machine learning workloads and batch ETL/ELT workloads?

options.A: Data lakehouse requires very little data modeling
options.B: Data lakehouse combines compute and storage for simple governance
options.C: Data lakehouse provides autoscaling for compute clusters
options.D: Data lakehouse can store unstructured data and support ACID transactions
options.E: Data lakehouse fully exists in the cloud

questions.explanation: The ability to store unstructured data (images, text) needed for ML and enforce ACID transactions needed for reliable ETL makes the lakehouse suitable for both workload types.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a Databricks Lakehouse object?

options.A: Tables
options.B: Views
options.C: Database/Schemas
options.D: Catalog

questions.explanation: Databricks lakehouse objects include catalogs, databases/schemas, tables, views, and functions. Stored procedures are a traditional RDBMS concept not natively supported as a first-class object in Databricks SQL.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a correct statement about Delta Lake implementing a lakehouse?

options.A: Delta Lake uses proprietary format
options.B: Using Apache Hadoop on cloud object storage
options.C: Delta Lake always stores metadata in memory
options.D: Delta Lake uses open source, open format, optimized cloud storage, scalable metadata
options.E: Delta Lake stores data and metadata in compute memory

questions.explanation: Delta Lake is open source, uses open Parquet format for data, stores metadata in an open transaction log, and is optimized for cloud object storage with scalable metadata handling.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about the Databricks Lakehouse Platform is incorrect?

options.A: Supports end-to-end streaming and batch workloads
options.B: Supports ACID transactions
options.C: Supports diverse data types including structured and unstructured
options.D: Supports BI and machine learning

questions.explanation: A core lakehouse principle is the decoupling of storage from compute, which enables independent scaling and cost efficiency.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes the Bronze layer in the Medallion Architecture?

options.A: Contains aggregated, business-ready data optimized for analytics
options.B: Contains validated and cleaned data with applied business rules
options.C: Contains raw, unvalidated data preserving the original format from source systems
options.D: Contains metadata and schema definitions for all data assets
options.E: Contains only streaming data ingested in real-time

questions.explanation: The Bronze layer is the landing zone for raw ingested data in its original format. It serves as the single source of truth for reprocessing and auditing.

questions.text: A data workload needs to use a Bronze table as its destination. Which of the following describes this workload?

options.A: Job aggregating cleaned data for summary statistics
options.B: Job querying aggregated data for a dashboard
options.C: Job ingesting raw data from a streaming source into the Lakehouse
options.D: Job developing a feature set for machine learning
options.E: Job enriching data by parsing timestamps

questions.explanation: Bronze tables receive raw ingested data — this is the raw ingestion job landing data from a source system.

questions.text: A data workload uses a Silver table as its source. Which of the following best describes this workload?

options.A: Job enriching data by parsing timestamps
options.B: Job querying aggregated data feeding a dashboard
options.C: Job ingesting raw data from a streaming source
options.D: Job aggregating cleaned data to create summary statistics
options.E: Job cleaning data by removing malformatted records

questions.explanation: Silver tables contain validated, cleaned data. A job reading from Silver and creating aggregations is producing Gold-layer data, making Silver the source for aggregation workloads.

questions.text: A data workload uses a Gold table as its source. Which of the following best describes this workload?

options.A: Job enriching data by parsing timestamps
options.B: Job querying aggregated data that already feeds into a dashboard
options.C: Job ingesting raw data from a streaming source
options.D: Job aggregating cleaned data for summary statistics
options.E: Job cleaning data by removing malformatted records

questions.explanation: Gold tables contain business-ready, aggregated data. Dashboard queries are the primary consumers of Gold-layer data.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about Databricks Notebooks?

options.A: Notebooks only support Python
options.B: Notebooks do not support real-time collaboration
options.C: Databricks Notebooks support real-time coauthoring on a single notebook
options.D: Notebooks require a separate Git client to be versioned
options.E: Notebooks can only be scheduled through the Jobs API

questions.explanation: Databricks Notebooks support real-time coauthoring, allowing multiple users to edit the same notebook simultaneously with live cursor tracking.

questions.text: What is the best approach when multiple engineers need to use both SQL and Python within the same notebook?

options.A: Create two separate notebooks, one for SQL and one for Python
options.B: A single notebook can support multiple languages using magic commands (e.g., %sql, %python)
options.C: Use an all-purpose cluster for Python and a SQL endpoint for SQL
options.D: Use a job cluster for Python and a SQL endpoint for SQL

questions.explanation: Magic commands like %sql, %python, %scala, and %r allow switching languages within a single Databricks notebook cell.

questions.text: Which of the following describes an advantage of using Databricks Repos over traditional notebook versioning?

options.A: Merge when code is committed
options.B: Pull request and review process
options.C: Trigger Databricks Repos API to pull the latest version of code into production
options.D: Resolve merge conflicts

questions.explanation: A key CI/CD advantage of Databricks Repos is the ability to trigger the Repos API to programmatically pull the latest production code, enabling automated deployment pipelines.

questions.text: When should a data team utilize cluster pools?

options.A: An automated report needs to be refreshed as quickly as possible
options.B: An automated report needs to be made reproducible
options.C: An automated report needs to be tested to identify errors
options.D: An automated report needs to be version-controlled across collaborators
options.E: An automated report needs to be runnable by all stakeholders

questions.explanation: Cluster pools maintain pre-allocated, idle instances so clusters can start almost immediately without waiting for VM provisioning, reducing startup time significantly.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the best cluster type to use for a regularly scheduled nightly reporting job that needs to populate a table?

options.A: All-purpose cluster for a single job
options.B: Job cluster
options.C: Use an Azure VM to read and write Delta tables
options.D: Use a Delta Live Table pipeline in continuous mode

questions.explanation: Job clusters are created when the job runs and terminated when it completes. They are more cost-effective than all-purpose clusters for automated workloads since they don't sit idle.

questions.text: Which of the following benefits is provided by array functions in Spark SQL?

options.A: Ability to work with multiple data types simultaneously
options.B: Ability to work with data within partitions and windows
options.C: Ability to work with time-related data in intervals
options.D: An ability to work with complex, nested data ingested from JSON files

questions.explanation: Array functions (explode, flatten, collect_list, etc.) are designed to work with complex, nested data structures such as arrays and structs commonly found in JSON and semi-structured sources.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a benefit of the Photon engine in Databricks?

options.A: Photon enables real-time notebook collaboration
options.B: Photon provides a vectorized query engine that accelerates SQL and DataFrame operations
options.C: Photon is used exclusively for machine learning model training
options.D: Photon replaces the Delta transaction log with an in-memory structure

questions.explanation: Photon is Databricks' native vectorized query engine written in C++ that significantly accelerates SQL and DataFrame operations, especially for scan-heavy workloads.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about the Databricks workspace (data plane) in the classic architecture?

options.A: The data plane contains the Databricks web application
options.B: The data plane hosts the cluster management service
options.C: The data plane is located in the customer's cloud account and runs the actual compute (driver and worker nodes) and stores data
options.D: The data plane is managed entirely by Databricks

questions.explanation: In the classic Databricks architecture, the data plane (customer cloud account) contains the actual VMs (driver and worker nodes), DBFS, and cloud storage. The control plane (Databricks account) manages orchestration.

questions.text: In a multi-hop (medallion) architecture, which of the following is a key advantage of the Gold layer over the Silver layer?

options.A: Gold tables store raw data for reprocessing
options.B: Gold tables are optimized for specific business use cases with aggregations and denormalization, providing faster BI query performance
options.C: Gold tables use a different file format than Silver tables
options.D: Gold tables can only be read by data engineers, not data analysts

questions.explanation: Gold tables are pre-aggregated, denormalized, and optimized for specific analytical use cases. They provide faster query performance for BI tools and dashboards compared to Silver tables which still retain normalized structures.

questions.text: Which of the following is a key difference between Bronze and raw data tables?

options.A: Bronze tables must be in Parquet format; raw tables can be in any format
options.B: Bronze tables are Delta format with full ACID support and history, while raw tables may be original source files without governance
options.C: Raw tables support time travel; Bronze tables do not
options.D: Bronze tables cannot store unstructured data

questions.explanation: In the lakehouse/medallion architecture, Bronze tables are managed Delta tables that add ACID compliance, schema enforcement, and transaction history on top of raw ingested data, which may be unmanaged flat files in arbitrary formats.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes what the Databricks Lakehouse Platform provides that a traditional data warehouse does not?

options.A: SQL query support
options.B: Data visualization and dashboarding
options.C: Support for storing and processing unstructured data such as images and text alongside structured data
options.D: Role-based access control

questions.explanation: Traditional data warehouses are designed for structured data only. The lakehouse extends this to support all data types (structured, semi-structured, unstructured) in a unified platform. ## Domain 2: ELT with Spark SQL and Python

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer is attempting to drop a Spark SQL table called my_table using DROP TABLE IF EXISTS my_table. After running this command, the engineer notices that the data files and metadata files have been deleted from the file system. Which of the following describes why all of these files were deleted?

options.A: The table was managed
options.B: The table's data was smaller than 10 GB
options.C: The table's data was larger than 10 GB
options.D: The table was external
options.E: The table did not have a location

questions.explanation: When a managed table is dropped, Databricks deletes both the metadata and the underlying data files. Dropping an external table only removes the metadata; the data files remain.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer has been given a new record: id STRING = 'a1', rank INTEGER = 6, rating FLOAT = 9.4. Which of the following SQL commands can be used to append the new record to an existing Delta table my_table?

options.A: INSERT INTO my_table VALUES ('a1', 6, 9.4)
options.B: my_table UNION VALUES ('a1', 6, 9.4)
options.C: INSERT VALUES ('a1', 6, 9.4) INTO my_table
options.D: UPDATE my_table VALUES ('a1', 6, 9.4)

questions.explanation: INSERT INTO is the standard SQL command for appending rows to an existing table. The syntax INSERT INTO table VALUES (...) is correct.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer has created a new database using CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS customer360. In which of the following locations will the customer360 database be located?

options.A: dbfs:/user/hive/database/customer360
options.B: dbfs:/user/hive/warehouse
options.C: dbfs:/user/hive/customer360
options.D: More information is needed to determine the correct response

questions.explanation: Without specifying a LOCATION, managed databases are stored in the default warehouse directory. However, this location depends on the workspace configuration (DBFS root, Unity Catalog managed storage, external Hive metastore), so more context is needed.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is the default file format used when writing a Delta table in Databricks?

options.A: Parquet
options.B: JSON
options.C: Delta
options.D: CSV

questions.explanation: When writing a DataFrame with .write.format("delta"), or creating a table in Databricks with no format specified in newer workspaces, Delta is the default format.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following creates a Delta table from a DataFrame in PySpark?

options.A: df.write.format("parquet").saveAsTable("my_table")
options.B: df.write.saveAsTable("my_table")
options.C: df.write.format("delta").saveAsTable("my_table")
options.D: df.saveAsTable("my_table")

questions.explanation: Explicitly specifying format("delta") ensures the table is written in Delta format with a transaction log.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which SQL command creates a managed Delta table?

options.A: CREATE DELTA TABLE my_table
options.B: CREATE TABLE my_table USING DELTA
options.C: CREATE MANAGED TABLE my_table
options.D: CREATE DELTA MANAGED TABLE my_table

questions.explanation: The standard DDL for creating a managed Delta table is CREATE TABLE table_name USING DELTA (optionally with column definitions and partitioning).

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is the main difference between a managed table and an external table in Databricks?

options.A: Managed tables support more file formats
options.B: External tables cannot be queried via SQL
options.C: Managed tables store data in the metastore-managed location; dropping them removes data
options.D: External tables lack Delta Lake support

questions.explanation: Managed tables have their lifecycle tied to the metastore: dropping the table removes both metadata and data files. External tables only have metadata managed; data files are preserved on drop.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer creates a table using the LOCATION keyword. What type of table is created?

options.A: Managed Delta table
options.B: External table
options.C: Managed table
options.D: Temporary table

questions.explanation: Using LOCATION in CREATE TABLE specifies an external path, making it an external (unmanaged) table. The data files at that path are not controlled by Databricks metadata lifecycle.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which SQL statement eliminates duplicate rows from query results?

options.A: SELECT DISTINCT * FROM table_name
options.B: SELECT DISTINCT * FROM table_name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
options.C: SELECT DISTINCT_ROWS (*) FROM table_name
options.D: SELECT * FROM table_name GROUP BY * HAVING COUNT(*) < 1

questions.explanation: SELECT DISTINCT eliminates duplicate rows from the result set.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Two tables exist: march_transactions and april_transactions with no duplicate rows between them. Which SQL command creates an all_transactions table combining both without duplicates?

options.A: INNER JOIN
options.B: UNION
options.C: OUTER JOIN
options.D: INTERSECT

questions.explanation: UNION (without ALL) deduplicates rows from both tables. Since there are no duplicates between the tables, the result contains all rows from both. UNION ALL would also work since there are no duplicates, but UNION is the safer choice to guarantee no duplicates.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does the .option("mergeSchema", "true") write option do?

options.A: Forces schema overwrite on every write
options.B: Merges new schema fields into the existing table schema
options.C: Prevents any schema changes
options.D: Drops columns not present in the new DataFrame

questions.explanation: mergeSchema allows schema evolution by adding new columns from the DataFrame to the existing Delta table schema without failing or overwriting existing columns.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does setting overwriteSchema to true do when writing to a Delta table?

options.A: The table is dropped and recreated
options.B: The schema is updated to match the DataFrame schema
options.C: The table data is deleted
options.D: Data is appended with new columns added

questions.explanation: overwriteSchema=true allows replacing the existing table schema entirely when writing in overwrite mode, useful when the structure has fundamentally changed.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following correctly creates a generated column in a Delta table DDL?

options.A: orderdate date GENERATED ALWAYS AS (CAST(orderTime as DATE))
options.B: orderdate date COMPUTED ALWAYS AS (CAST(orderTime as DATE))
options.C: orderdate date GENERATED COLUMN (CAST(orderTime as DATE))
options.D: orderdate date CAST orderTime to DATE

questions.explanation: The correct syntax for a generated column in Delta Lake is: column_name type GENERATED ALWAYS AS (expression).

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does the OPTIMIZE command do in Delta Lake?

options.A: Rewrites metadata files only
options.B: Compresses table data files using GZIP
options.C: Compacts small files into larger ones to improve read performance
options.D: Deletes null records from the table

questions.explanation: OPTIMIZE compacts small Parquet files produced by frequent small writes (streaming, incremental loads) into fewer larger files, significantly improving query scan performance.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is the purpose of Z-Ordering (ZORDER BY) in Delta Lake?

options.A: Encrypts the table files
options.B: Sorts files alphabetically for compression
options.C: Co-locates related data in the same files to optimize selective query reads via data skipping
options.D: Tracks schema version history

questions.explanation: Z-Ordering arranges data so that rows with similar values in the Z-ORDER columns are in the same files. Combined with Delta's min/max statistics, this enables file-level data skipping, greatly reducing I/O for selective queries.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is the purpose of the .repartition(n) transformation in Spark?

options.A: Increases available memory for a job
options.B: Sorts data by a key column
options.C: Reduces the number of tasks
options.D: Changes the number of partitions in a DataFrame

questions.explanation: .repartition(n) performs a full shuffle to redistribute data into exactly n partitions, useful for increasing parallelism or preparing data for downstream operations.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does the spark.sql.shuffle.partitions configuration control?

options.A: Sets the amount of shuffle memory
options.B: Controls the number of partitions used for shuffle operations (e.g., joins, aggregations)
options.C: Tracks shuffle operation progress
options.D: Limits the size of each shuffle file

questions.explanation: spark.sql.shuffle.partitions (default 200) determines the parallelism for wide transformations like joins and aggregations.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is one use of EXPLAIN in Spark SQL?

options.A: Encrypts query results
options.B: Cancels a running query
options.C: Displays the logical and physical query execution plan
options.D: Modifies the table schema

questions.explanation: EXPLAIN (or EXPLAIN EXTENDED) shows the query's logical and physical plan, useful for understanding how Spark will execute a query and identifying optimization opportunities.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is the correct syntax for creating a SQL UDF that converts temperature?

options.A: CREATE UDF FUNCTION udf_convert(temp DOUBLE, measure STRING) RETURNS DOUBLE ...
options.B: CREATE USER FUNCTION udf_convert(temp DOUBLE, measure STRING) ...
options.C: CREATE FUNCTION udf_convert(temp DOUBLE, measure STRING) RETURNS DOUBLE RETURN CASE WHEN measure = 'C' THEN (temp - 32) / 1.8 ELSE (temp * 1.8) + 32 END
options.D: CREATE TEMP FUNCTION udf_convert ...

questions.explanation: SQL UDFs in Databricks use CREATE FUNCTION with the RETURNS clause specifying the return type and RETURN followed by the expression.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: When calculating total sales from an ARRAY<STRUCT<employeeId: BIGINT, sales: INT>> column, which approach correctly aggregates the nested sales values?

options.A: WITH CTE using EXPLODE and SUM
options.B: select aggregate(flatten(collect_list(performance.sales)), 0, (x, y) -> x + y)
options.C: SELECT SUM(SLICE(performance, sales))
options.D: select reduce(performance.sales, 0, (x, y) -> x + y)

questions.explanation: collect_list collects the arrays, flatten combines them into a single array, and aggregate (reduce) sums the values. This correctly handles nested array-of-struct aggregation.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: In PySpark, what is the correct syntax for asserting that a row count equals 10?

options.A: assert (row_count = 10, 'Row count did not match')
options.B: assert if (row_count = 10, 'Row count did not match')
options.C: assert row_count == 10, 'Row count did not match'
options.D: assert if row_count == 10, 'Row count did not match'

questions.explanation: Python's assert statement uses == for equality comparison, with an optional error message after a comma: assert condition, message.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is the correct Python construct for graceful error handling when executing a Spark write operation?

options.A: try: ... failure: ...
options.B: try: ... catch: ...
options.C: try: ... except: ...
options.D: try: ... fail: ...

questions.explanation: Python uses try/except (not try/catch which is Java/Scala syntax) for exception handling.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What is the best approach for passing a batch date as a changing input parameter to a Databricks notebook?

options.A: Create a notebook parameter, assign to a Python variable, and use it with a Spark DataFrame filter
options.B: Create a dynamic view that calculates the batch date
options.C: There is no way to combine Python variables with Spark code
options.D: Manually edit the hardcoded date value each time the job runs

questions.explanation: Databricks widgets (dbutils.widgets) allow parameterizing notebooks. The widget value can be retrieved as a Python variable and used in Spark operations or SQL queries via spark.conf or string formatting.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is a correct statement about session-scoped temporary views in Databricks?

options.A: Temporary views are lost once the notebook is detached and re-attached to a cluster
options.B: Temporary views are stored on disk in memory
options.C: Temporary views are accessible after a cluster restart
options.D: Temporary views are created in the local_temp database

questions.explanation: Session-scoped temp views (CREATE TEMP VIEW) exist only for the duration of the SparkSession. Detaching and re-attaching a notebook creates a new session, so temp views are lost.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is a correct statement about global temporary views?

options.A: Cannot be accessed after notebook detach/re-attach
options.B: Are accessible across multiple clusters simultaneously
options.C: Are accessible even if the notebook is detached and re-attached (same cluster session)
options.D: Are accessible after a cluster restart

questions.explanation: Global temp views (CREATE GLOBAL TEMP VIEW) are stored in the global_temp database and persist for the life of the SparkContext (cluster session). They survive notebook detach/re-attach but are lost when the cluster restarts.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does INSERT OVERWRITE do to historical versions of a Delta table?

options.A: Overwrites data and clears all historical versions
options.B: Overwrites the current version but preserves historical versions, enabling time travel
options.C: Clears all historical data permanently
options.D: Appends data to the current version

questions.explanation: INSERT OVERWRITE replaces the current table data but records the operation in the Delta transaction log, so historical versions remain queryable via time travel. It differs from DROP + recreate which would lose history.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What format does Spark use by default when no format is specified for a write operation?

options.A: Delta
options.B: CSV
options.C: Parquet
options.D: Avro

questions.explanation: Spark's default write format is Parquet, not Delta. Delta is default in newer Databricks workspace configurations, but the underlying Spark default remains Parquet.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which file format supports schema evolution in Databricks Delta Lake?

options.A: CSV
options.B: JSON
options.C: Parquet
options.D: Delta

questions.explanation: Delta Lake has built-in schema evolution support via mergeSchema and overwriteSchema options. Raw Parquet, CSV, and JSON do not have managed schema evolution.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes what happens when DROP TABLE is run on an external Delta table?

options.A: Both the metadata and the data files are deleted
options.B: Only the metadata is removed from the metastore; data files remain in the external location
options.C: The table is renamed with a _deleted suffix
options.D: The data files are archived to a backup location
options.E: The operation fails because external tables cannot be dropped

questions.explanation: Dropping an external table only removes the table definition from the metastore. The underlying data files at the LOCATION path are preserved, unlike managed tables where both metadata and files are deleted.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: What does the DESCRIBE EXTENDED command provide for a Delta table?

options.A: Only the column names and data types
options.B: Detailed information including column names, data types, table type (managed vs external), location, provider, and table properties
options.C: Only the partition information
options.D: The last 10 transactions from the Delta log

questions.explanation: DESCRIBE EXTENDED (or DESCRIBE TABLE EXTENDED) provides comprehensive table metadata including schema, location path, table type, partition columns, table properties, and storage format.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is a true statement about Delta Lake ACID compliance?

options.A: Delta Lake ACID ensures standardized schema across all tables
options.B: Delta Lake provides better data compression than Parquet
options.C: Delta Lake's ACID compliance ensures reliable concurrent operations with consistent reads and writes
options.D: Delta Lake ACID compliance reduces cloud storage costs

questions.explanation: Delta Lake's ACID transactions (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) ensure that concurrent reads and writes behave correctly without data corruption or inconsistency.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer drops a managed table using DROP TABLE. Which of the following is also true?

options.A: Only the table definition in the metastore is removed; data files remain
options.B: The underlying data files AND the metastore entry are both deleted
options.C: The table is marked as deleted but recoverable for 30 days
options.D: The data files are moved to the recycle bin

questions.explanation: Dropping a managed table removes both the table metadata from the metastore AND the underlying data files from storage. This is the key difference from external tables.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: Which of the following is a true statement about Z-Ordering and its impact on query performance?

options.A: Z-Ordering physically sorts the entire table by the specified columns
options.B: Z-Ordering co-locates related data in files and enables file-level data skipping when filtering on Z-Ordered columns
options.C: Z-Ordering is equivalent to partitioning and can replace it
options.D: Z-Ordering only works on string columns

questions.explanation: Z-Ordering clusters related data into the same files. Combined with Delta's per-file min/max statistics, the query engine can skip entire files when filtering, dramatically reducing I/O.

questions.domainName: ELT with Spark SQL and Python
questions.text: A data engineer wants to create a table that should only be accessible while their Spark session is active, without persisting to any metastore. Which type of table/view should they create?

options.A: External table
options.B: Managed Delta table
options.C: Session-scoped temporary view (CREATE TEMP VIEW)
options.D: Global temporary view

questions.explanation: Session-scoped temp views (CREATE TEMP VIEW or CREATE TEMPORARY VIEW) exist only in the current SparkSession and are not persisted to any metastore, making them invisible to other sessions and clusters. ## Domain 3: Incremental Data Processing

questions.text: Which of the following describes the type of workloads that are always compatible with Auto Loader?

options.A: Streaming workloads
options.B: Machine learning workloads
options.C: Serverless workloads
options.D: Batch workloads

questions.explanation: Auto Loader uses Structured Streaming under the hood and is always streaming-compatible. It reads from cloud storage using the cloudFiles source and processes data incrementally.

questions.text: A data engineer is designing a pipeline where the source generates files in a shared directory. Files accumulate and should not be deleted. The engineer needs to ingest only new files on each run. Which tool solves this problem?

options.A: Unity Catalog
options.B: Delta Lake
options.C: Databricks SQL
options.D: Data Explorer

questions.explanation: Auto Loader tracks which files have been processed using checkpoints and metadata logs, enabling incremental ingestion of only new files without modifying or deleting the source files.

questions.text: What is the key difference between Auto Loader and COPY INTO for file ingestion?

options.A: Auto Loader supports file notification when performing incremental loads
options.B: COPY INTO is more efficient for large volumes of millions of files
options.C: Auto Loader only supports directory listing mode
options.D: COPY INTO supports file notification

questions.explanation: Auto Loader supports both directory listing and file notification (via cloud event services like AWS SQS or Azure Event Grid) for detecting new files. COPY INTO only supports directory listing and is better suited for smaller file sets.

questions.text: Why does Auto Loader require a schema location to be configured?

options.A: The schema location stores the schema inferred by Auto Loader across job restarts
options.B: To improve data compression ratios
options.C: For fine-grained access control on the data
options.D: To enable result caching

questions.explanation: Auto Loader infers schema from sampled files and stores it in the schema location (_schemas directory) to avoid re-inferring on every stream restart, ensuring schema stability.

questions.text: Which API is used to perform a streaming read with Auto Loader?

options.A: .read()
options.B: .read.format("delta")
options.C: .readStream.format("cloudFiles")
options.D: .readStream()

questions.explanation: Auto Loader uses the cloudFiles source format with the Structured Streaming readStream API: spark.readStream.format("cloudFiles").option("cloudFiles.format", "json")...

questions.text: What supported methods does Auto Loader use to identify the arrival of new files?

options.A: Directory listing and file notification
options.B: Checkpointing and watermarking
options.C: Write-ahead logging and read-head logging
options.D: File hashing and dynamic file lookup

questions.explanation: Auto Loader uses two file detection modes: directory listing (polls the directory for new files) and file notification (uses cloud storage event notifications for scalable, near-real-time detection).

questions.text: How does Auto Loader track which files have already been processed?

options.A: By tracking file names in a list
options.B: By comparing file timestamps
options.C: Through checkpoint and metadata logs stored in the checkpoint location
options.D: By comparing file sizes

questions.explanation: Auto Loader writes a checkpoint and maintains metadata logs in the configured checkpointLocation, enabling exactly-once processing and resuming from where it left off after failures.

questions.text: What does .trigger(once=True) do in a Structured Streaming query?

options.A: Disables the streaming job permanently
options.B: Processes all available data as one batch, then stops the stream
options.C: Triggers the stream every second
options.D: Runs the stream indefinitely

questions.explanation: trigger(once=True) processes all available data as a single micro-batch and then terminates the stream, useful for scheduled batch-style incremental updates.

questions.text: A data engineer wants a Structured Streaming query to execute a micro-batch every 5 seconds. Which trigger configuration is correct?

options.A: .trigger("5 seconds")
options.B: .trigger()
options.C: .trigger(once="5 seconds")
options.D: .trigger(processingTime="5 seconds")

questions.explanation: trigger(processingTime="5 seconds") sets a fixed interval between micro-batch processing. trigger(continuous=...) enables continuous processing mode (experimental).

questions.text: What tracks the versions of a Delta table's transaction history?

options.A: JSON log files in a separate S3 bucket
options.B: Versioned CSV files in the table directory
options.C: The transaction log directory (_delta_log) containing JSON commit files and checkpoint files
options.D: The Hive Metastore database

questions.explanation: Delta Lake maintains a _delta_log directory within the table directory. Each transaction creates a numbered JSON file (000.json, 001.json, etc.) and periodic Parquet checkpoint files recording the table's state.

questions.text: Which SQL command shows the full history of transactions for a Delta table?

options.A: SHOW VERSIONS FOR table_name
options.B: DESCRIBE HISTORY table_name
options.C: SHOW LOGS FOR table_name
options.D: LIST DELTA VERSIONS table_name

questions.explanation: DESCRIBE HISTORY table_name returns a history of all operations performed on the table including timestamps, user, operation type, and version number.

questions.text: Which Delta Lake feature allows a data engineer to simplify the backup process without manually copying files before an update operation?

options.A: Use the time travel feature to refer to old data instead of manually copying
options.B: Use DEEP CLONE to create a backup before the update
options.C: Use SHADOW COPY as a backup strategy
options.D: Cloud storage automatically retains previous file versions

questions.explanation: Delta's time travel (version history) means previous versions of data remain queryable via VERSION AS OF or TIMESTAMP AS OF — no manual backup copying needed.

questions.text: What is the impact of running VACUUM transactions RETAIN 0 HOURS on a Delta table?

options.A: Command succeeds and removes no data
options.B: Command fails if there is an active transaction running on the table
options.C: Command fails because it cannot run with the retention duration check enabled (default safety threshold is 7 days)
options.D: Command succeeds and removes all historical data immediately

questions.explanation: By default, Delta Lake prevents VACUUM with a retention period less than 7 days (168 hours) to protect time travel. To override, you must set spark.databricks.delta.retentionDurationCheck.enabled = false.

questions.text: What happens when you query a Delta table while another write operation is actively updating it?

options.A: The query fails with a lock conflict error
options.B: The query waits until the write operation completes
options.C: The query reads a consistent snapshot from the transaction log (snapshot isolation)
options.D: The query returns partial results from the in-progress write

questions.explanation: Delta Lake's ACID transactions with snapshot isolation ensure readers always see a consistent state without blocking writes. Concurrent reads and writes are supported.

questions.text: Which techniques provide end-to-end fault tolerance in Structured Streaming?

options.A: Checkpointing and watermarking
options.B: Write-ahead logging and watermarking
options.C: Checkpointing and idempotent sinks
options.D: Write-ahead logging and idempotent sinks

questions.explanation: Structured Streaming achieves fault tolerance through: (1) checkpointing the stream progress and state, and (2) idempotent sinks (exactly-once writes, e.g., using Delta Lake). These two together guarantee end-to-end exactly-once semantics.

questions.text: What is the main purpose of the checkpoint location in a Structured Streaming query?

options.A: To improve write speed to the sink
options.B: To track data quality metrics
options.C: To maintain stream state and track progress for fault tolerance and restart recovery
options.D: To create automatic data backups

questions.explanation: The checkpoint location stores the stream's current offset (progress) and any stateful aggregation state. This allows the stream to recover from failures and resume from where it left off.

questions.text: How do you ensure idempotent writes in a Structured Streaming pipeline?

options.A: Disable checkpointing
options.B: Use append output mode only
options.C: Use complete output mode
options.D: Enable checkpointing and use deduplication at the sink (e.g., Delta Lake with idempotent writes)

questions.explanation: Checkpointing tracks offsets to avoid reprocessing, and Delta Lake's transaction log enables idempotent (exactly-once) writes even on retry, together ensuring end-to-end idempotency.

questions.text: Which command is used to create a streaming view on a Delta table in PySpark?

options.A: spark.read.format("delta").table("my_table").createOrReplaceTempView("my_view")
options.B: spark.readStream.format("delta").table("my_table").createOrReplaceTempView("my_view")
options.C: spark.read.format("delta").table("my_table").mode("stream").createOrReplaceTempView("my_view")
options.D: spark.read.format("delta").stream("my_table").createOrReplaceTempView("my_view")

questions.explanation: To create a streaming temp view from a Delta table, use readStream (not read) so Spark processes it as a streaming source.

questions.text: What does the .foreachBatch() method do in Structured Streaming?

options.A: Writes the entire stream once and stops
options.B: Triggers the stream processing manually
options.C: Applies custom processing logic to each micro-batch as a static DataFrame
options.D: Skips writing to the output sink

questions.explanation: .foreachBatch() receives each micro-batch as a static DataFrame, enabling complex operations like MERGE, multi-table writes, or calling ML model inference that aren't directly supported in streaming mode.

questions.text: Which of the following file formats is required by the COPY INTO command?

options.A: Delta only
options.B: JSON only
options.C: CSV only
options.D: Any supported format (JSON, CSV, Parquet, Avro, etc.)

questions.explanation: COPY INTO supports multiple file formats including JSON, CSV, Parquet, Avro, ORC, and text. The format is specified with the FILEFORMAT clause.

questions.text: What are the two output modes compatible with watermarks in Structured Streaming?

options.A: Complete and append
options.B: Append and update
options.C: Complete and update
options.D: All three modes work with watermarks

questions.explanation: Watermarks work with append mode (produces final results after watermark passes) and update mode (updates aggregates as new data arrives). Complete mode emits the entire result set each time, so watermarks are not compatible with it.

questions.text: Which of the following is a benefit of using DEEP CLONE in Delta Lake?

options.A: Creates a zero-copy reference to the source table's files
options.B: Creates an independent full copy of the source table's data and metadata, useful for backups and testing
options.C: Clones the schema only without copying data files
options.D: Creates a read-only snapshot view of the table

questions.explanation: DEEP CLONE copies both the data files and the transaction log to a new location, creating a fully independent copy. This is useful for creating production backups or test environments. SHALLOW CLONE is the zero-copy reference approach.

questions.text: A data engineer runs VACUUM on a Delta table. Which of the following is a consequence of this operation?

options.A: All table data is permanently deleted
options.B: Old data files outside the retention period are deleted, which can break time travel for versions before the retention boundary
options.C: The transaction log is cleared
options.D: Table statistics are recomputed

questions.explanation: VACUUM removes data files no longer referenced by the current or recent transaction log entries (outside the retention period, default 7 days). This frees storage but means you can no longer time-travel to versions whose files were vacuumed.

questions.text: Which of the following SQL commands restores a Delta table to a previous version?

options.A: RESTORE TABLE sales VERSION AS OF 24
options.B: ROLLBACK TABLE sales TO VERSION 24
options.C: REVERT TABLE sales VERSION 24
options.D: RESET TABLE sales TO VERSION AS OF 24

questions.explanation: The RESTORE TABLE command in Delta Lake restores the table to a specified historical version (VERSION AS OF) or timestamp (TIMESTAMP AS OF).

questions.text: A data engineer creates a SHALLOW CLONE of a Delta table. What happens when VACUUM is run on the shallow clone?

options.A: VACUUM removes files from both the clone and the source table
options.B: VACUUM on the shallow clone results in an error because shallow clones reference the source table's files
options.C: VACUUM removes only the clone's metadata files
options.D: VACUUM on a shallow clone behaves identically to running VACUUM on a regular table

questions.explanation: SHALLOW CLONE does not copy data files — it references the source table's files. Running VACUUM on a shallow clone can result in errors or unexpected behavior because the referenced files are managed by the source table.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the processingTime configuration in a Structured Streaming query?

options.A: Sets the maximum time a stream can run before auto-terminating
options.B: Controls the interval between micro-batch processing triggers
options.C: Sets the timeout for each individual record processing
options.D: Configures the stream's checkpoint write interval

questions.explanation: processingTime in the trigger configuration (e.g., trigger(processingTime="30 seconds")) specifies the interval between consecutive micro-batch executions.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a benefit of configuring continuous processing mode in Structured Streaming?

options.A: Processes data exactly once per day
options.B: Provides lower latency by processing records as soon as they arrive with millisecond latency
options.C: Eliminates the need for checkpointing
options.D: Automatically scales compute based on data volume

questions.explanation: Continuous processing mode (experimental) processes records with ~1ms latency instead of the seconds typical for micro-batch. However, it has limited operator support compared to micro-batch mode.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes when COPY INTO should be used instead of Auto Loader?

options.A: When processing millions of files continuously arriving in cloud storage
options.B: When schema evolution is required
options.C: When loading a relatively small, bounded set of files that don't require schema inference
options.D: When near-real-time latency is required

questions.explanation: COPY INTO is best for idempotent, bounded batch loads from cloud storage. Auto Loader is preferred when dealing with continuous file arrivals, large volumes (millions of files), or when schema inference/evolution is needed.

questions.text: What happens to a Delta table's VACUUM when a shallow clone exists that references the source table's files?

options.A: VACUUM runs normally and removes unreferenced files
options.B: VACUUM on the source could delete files still referenced by the shallow clone, potentially breaking the clone
options.C: VACUUM is blocked until all shallow clones are deleted
options.D: VACUUM automatically excludes files referenced by shallow clones

questions.explanation: Shallow clones reference the source table's data files without copying them. Running VACUUM on the source table may delete files that the shallow clone still depends on, because VACUUM doesn't know about external references.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct way to reference a previous Delta table version for time travel?

options.A: SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE version = 5
options.B: SELECT * FROM my_table VERSION AS OF 5
options.C: SELECT * FROM my_table AT VERSION 5
options.D: SELECT * FROM my_table SNAPSHOT VERSION 5

questions.explanation: Delta Lake time travel syntax uses VERSION AS OF n (or TIMESTAMP AS OF '2024-01-01') in the SELECT statement or using the @v notation: my_table@v5.

questions.text: What is the minimum retention period for VACUUM to work without disabling the safety check in Databricks?

options.A: 24 hours
options.B: 48 hours
options.C: 168 hours (7 days)
options.D: 30 days

questions.explanation: Delta Lake's default minimum retention period for VACUUM is 168 hours (7 days). Running VACUUM with a shorter retention requires explicitly disabling the delta.retentionDurationCheck safety check.

questions.text: Which of the following describes how Structured Streaming handles late-arriving data?

options.A: Late data is always dropped and cannot be processed
options.B: Watermarks define a threshold; data arriving after the watermark is typically dropped
options.C: All late data is buffered indefinitely until processed
options.D: Late data is automatically replayed from the source

questions.explanation: Watermarks define how late data can be and still be processed. Data arriving after the watermark threshold is dropped from aggregation windows. The watermark threshold must be set to balance latency vs. data completeness.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the best approach to prevent the same data from being ingested twice when using COPY INTO?

options.A: COPY INTO is not idempotent; you must track processed files manually
options.B: COPY INTO is idempotent by design — running the same COPY INTO command multiple times on the same data will not duplicate records
options.C: Add a DISTINCT clause to the COPY INTO statement
options.D: Use a watermark to prevent duplicate ingestion

questions.explanation: COPY INTO tracks which files have already been loaded (using internal state) and skips them on subsequent runs, making it idempotent. Running the same COPY INTO command multiple times is safe.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `_rescued_data` column that Auto Loader can add to a DataFrame?

options.A: It stores the file name of each ingested record
options.B: It captures data that doesn't match the schema (malformed records, extra columns) so no data is lost
options.C: It stores the checksum of each ingested file
options.D: It captures records that were deduplicated

questions.explanation: When schema enforcement is enabled, Auto Loader stores data that doesn't match the declared schema in a _rescued_data column as a JSON string, preventing data loss from schema mismatches.

questions.text: What is the function of `spark.readStream.format("delta").table("my_table")` in PySpark?

options.A: Reads the entire Delta table as a static batch DataFrame
options.B: Creates a streaming DataFrame that reads new records appended to the Delta table incrementally
options.C: Creates a real-time connection to an external streaming source
options.D: Reads the latest snapshot of the Delta table as a stream

questions.explanation: Using readStream with a Delta table source creates a streaming DataFrame that processes only newly appended records since the last checkpoint, enabling incremental downstream processing. ## Domain 4: Production Pipelines

questions.text: Which of the following data quality monitoring tools in Databricks is designed for monitoring data quality and automatically quarantining bad data?

options.A: Auto Loader
options.B: Delta Live Tables
options.C: Jobs and Tasks
options.D: Unity Catalog

questions.explanation: Delta Live Tables (DLT) has built-in data quality expectations (CONSTRAINT / EXPECT clauses) that allow tagging, dropping, or failing on invalid records, with quality metrics tracked in the event log.

questions.text: A dataset has been defined using Delta Live Tables with the following: CONSTRAINT valid_timestamp EXPECT (timestamp > '2020-01-01') ON VIOLATION DROP ROW. What is the expected behavior when a batch of data containing violations is processed?

options.A: Records are dropped and loaded into a quarantine table
options.B: Records are added to the dataset with an invalid flag
options.C: Records are dropped and recorded as invalid in the event log
options.D: Records are added to the dataset and recorded in the event log

questions.explanation: ON VIOLATION DROP ROW causes invalid records to be silently dropped from the target dataset. The violations are recorded (count of dropped rows, etc.) in the DLT event log for monitoring.

questions.text: When should CREATE STREAMING LIVE TABLE be used over CREATE LIVE TABLE in Delta Live Tables?

options.A: When the subsequent step in the pipeline is static
options.B: When data needs to be processed incrementally from a streaming or append-only source
options.C: The syntax is redundant and unnecessary
options.D: When performing complicated aggregations
options.E: When the previous step in the pipeline is static

questions.explanation: CREATE STREAMING LIVE TABLE processes data incrementally (new rows only) from streaming sources. CREATE LIVE TABLE (materialized view) recomputes the entire result from scratch on each pipeline update.

questions.text: In a Delta Live Tables query that references STREAM(LIVE.customers), why is the STREAM() function needed?

options.A: It is not needed and will cause an error
options.B: Because the customers table was updated since the last run
options.C: Because the customers table is itself a Streaming Live Table
options.D: Because it references a Structured Streaming PySpark DataFrame

questions.explanation: When reading from another DLT table that is a STREAMING LIVE TABLE, you must use STREAM(LIVE.table_name) to tell DLT to process it as a stream (incrementally) rather than recomputing from scratch.

questions.text: What must be specified when creating a new Delta Live Tables pipeline?

options.A: A key-value pair configuration for cluster settings
options.B: At least one notebook library to execute
options.C: A cloud storage location path for written data
options.D: A target database location for data

questions.explanation: The minimum requirement for a DLT pipeline is at least one source notebook containing the pipeline definitions (CREATE LIVE TABLE / CREATE STREAMING LIVE TABLE statements).

questions.text: How can a data engineer identify which table in a DLT pipeline is dropping records?

options.A: Set up separate expectations for each table
options.B: It cannot be determined without external logging
options.C: Set up email notifications for each table
options.D: Navigate to the DLT pipeline UI, click each table, and view the data quality statistics panel
options.E: Click the "Error" button on the pipeline page to review errors

questions.explanation: The DLT pipeline UI provides a graphical view of each table with data quality metrics (records passed, failed, dropped). Clicking on a table shows its constraint statistics.

questions.text: What is the main difference between CREATE STREAMING LIVE TABLE and CREATE LIVE TABLE in DLT?

options.A: STREAMING LIVE TABLE is used in multi-hop architecture only
options.B: LIVE TABLE is used for streaming data sources
options.C: STREAMING LIVE TABLE processes streaming sources and incremental data; LIVE TABLE recomputes from all data
options.D: They are functionally identical; STREAMING LIVE TABLE syntax is deprecated
options.E: They use different underlying processing engines

questions.explanation: STREAMING LIVE TABLE (streaming table) processes only new/appended data incrementally. LIVE TABLE (materialized view) recomputes from the complete source dataset each pipeline run.

questions.text: A data engineer's job fails randomly because of spot instance interruptions. Which feature in Databricks Jobs/Tasks can address this issue?

options.A: Add a retry policy to the task
options.B: Switch all compute to on-demand instances
options.C: Use multiple clusters in parallel
options.D: Enable cluster autoscaling

questions.explanation: Configuring a retry policy on a task allows it to automatically retry on failure (e.g., from a spot instance interruption) without manual intervention, improving job resilience.

questions.text: A Databricks Job has a single task. A new task is needed that runs a notebook BEFORE the original task. How should the engineer set this up?

options.A: Clone the existing task and update the notebook path
options.B: Create a new task and add the original task as a dependency of the new task
options.C: Create a new task with the original task as a dependent
options.D: Create a new Job with both tasks running concurrently
options.E: Clone the job to a new Job and edit the notebook

questions.explanation: In Databricks Jobs, task dependencies flow from prerequisite to dependent. To make Task A run before Task B, you configure Task B to "depend on" Task A. So create the new (prerequisite) task and make the original task depend on it.

questions.text: When should a Task be selected in the "Depends On" field of a Databricks Job task configuration?

options.A: When another task needs to be replaced by this task
options.B: When another task must complete successfully before this task begins
options.C: When another task shares dependency libraries with this task
options.D: When another task uses minimal compute resources

questions.explanation: "Depends On" creates an execution ordering dependency. The task will only start after all tasks listed in its "Depends On" field complete successfully.

questions.text: A data team has a multi-task Databricks Job that runs nightly, but cluster startup is taking too long. How can cluster startup time be improved?

options.A: Use Databricks SQL endpoints instead of clusters
options.B: Use job clusters instead of all-purpose clusters
options.C: Configure single-node clusters for all tasks
options.D: Use clusters from a pre-configured cluster pool
options.E: Configure autoscaling for larger data volumes

questions.explanation: Cluster pools maintain a set of ready-to-use, idle VM instances. When a job starts, it uses a pre-warmed VM from the pool, drastically reducing cluster startup time.

questions.text: Different ways to trigger a Databricks Job run include which of the following options?

options.A: Continuous and incremental
options.B: On-demand and file notification
options.C: Cron schedule and on-demand (manual) runs
options.D: Cron and file notification

questions.explanation: Databricks Jobs can be triggered either on a cron schedule (time-based) or manually (on-demand). File arrival triggers and continuous mode are features of DLT and streaming, not standard Jobs scheduling.

questions.text: Which location in the Databricks UI allows a data engineer to analyze slow Job performance over the past 60 days?

options.A: Under the Jobs UI, select the job, and under Runs view active runs and last 60 days of run history
options.B: Under the Jobs UI, select the job cluster, then access Spark UI
options.C: Under Workspace logs
options.D: Under the Compute UI
options.E: Historical runs are only accessible via the REST API

questions.explanation: The Jobs UI provides a run history view showing completed runs, duration, success/failure status, and logs for up to 60 days, enabling performance trend analysis.

questions.text: A manager wants a query to refresh every minute for exactly one week post-release, then stop automatically to avoid costs. What is the correct approach?

options.A: Set a DBU consumption limit on the SQL warehouse
options.B: Set the refresh schedule to end after a certain number of refreshes
options.C: It is impossible to guarantee costs will stop
options.D: Limit the number of individuals who can manage the refresh schedule
options.E: Set a refresh schedule end date in the query scheduler

questions.explanation: Databricks SQL query schedules support setting an end date/time. After the specified date, the schedule stops automatically without manual intervention.

questions.text: A dashboard refreshes once daily. Which approach minimizes the SQL warehouse's running time between refreshes?

options.A: Ensure the dashboard's SQL warehouse matches the one used for ad-hoc queries
options.B: Set the warehouse to serverless
options.C: Enable the Auto Stop feature on the SQL warehouse
options.D: Reduce the cluster size
options.E: Ensure the dashboard's SQL warehouse differs from ad-hoc query warehouses

questions.explanation: Auto Stop automatically terminates the SQL warehouse after a configured period of inactivity, minimizing idle costs between scheduled refreshes.

questions.text: How can you improve query performance when multiple users run concurrent parallel queries on a single SQL warehouse?

options.A: Turn on the Serverless feature
options.B: Increase the maximum bound of the warehouse's scaling range (scale-out)
options.C: Increase the cluster size from 2X-Small to 4XL (scale-up)
options.D: Turn on Auto Stop
options.E: Enable Serverless with a reliability-optimized policy

questions.explanation: For concurrent parallel queries, scaling out (more clusters) is the solution. Increasing max scaling range allows the warehouse to spin up additional clusters to handle concurrent queries.

questions.text: A single sequential query is taking 30 minutes on a 2X-Small SQL warehouse. How should performance be improved?

options.A: Turn on Serverless
options.B: Increase the maximum scaling range
options.C: Increase the cluster size from 2X-Small to 4XL (scale-up)
options.D: Turn on Auto Stop

questions.explanation: For a single slow sequential query, scaling up (larger cluster with more cores/memory) is the solution. Scale-out (more clusters) helps concurrent queries, not sequential single-query performance.

questions.text: Which Git operation must be performed outside of Databricks Repos and cannot be done within the Repos interface?

options.A: Commit
options.B: Pull
options.C: Merge
options.D: Clone

questions.explanation: Databricks Repos supports commit, push, pull, branch switching, and creating branches. Merge operations (especially conflict resolution during merges) must be performed in an external Git client or repository hosting service.

questions.text: Which of the following methods can be used to reduce cluster costs for a job that runs infrequently?

options.A: Use an all-purpose cluster with auto-termination enabled
options.B: Use job clusters that are created on demand and terminated when the job completes
options.C: Use a high-concurrency cluster shared across multiple teams
options.D: Enable cluster autoscaling with a minimum of 10 workers

questions.explanation: Job clusters are ephemeral — created when a job starts and terminated when it ends. This means you only pay for compute while the job runs, unlike all-purpose clusters which may sit idle.

questions.text: A data engineer notices that a job's tasks run sequentially when they could run in parallel. Which configuration in Databricks Jobs enables parallel task execution?

options.A: Enable multi-cluster mode in the job settings
options.B: Configure tasks with no mutual dependencies so they can run concurrently
options.C: Increase the job cluster's number of worker nodes
options.D: Use a high-concurrency all-purpose cluster

questions.explanation: Tasks in Databricks Jobs run in parallel when they have no dependency relationship between them. Structuring the DAG so independent tasks have no "Depends On" relationship enables parallel execution.

questions.text: Which of the following best explains why Databricks recommends using job clusters over all-purpose clusters for automated production workloads?

options.A: Job clusters support more programming languages
options.B: All-purpose clusters do not support Delta Lake
options.C: Job clusters are created fresh for each run (no state contamination) and are terminated when done (lower cost)
options.D: Job clusters have higher memory limits

questions.explanation: Job clusters provide isolation (fresh environment per run), cost efficiency (no idle time), and prevent state contamination between runs, making them ideal for production pipelines.

questions.text: A data team wants to reduce project costs. Which of the following strategies would reduce compute costs?

options.A: Use an all-purpose cluster for all workloads
options.B: Use job clusters for automated workloads and configure auto-termination on interactive clusters
options.C: Increase cluster size to process faster
options.D: Use more partitions to improve parallelism

questions.explanation: Job clusters auto-terminate after job completion. For interactive clusters, setting auto-termination prevents paying for idle compute. Both approaches significantly reduce unnecessary compute spend.

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE about Databricks SQL serverless warehouses?

options.A: Serverless warehouses are only available for streaming workloads
options.B: Serverless warehouses eliminate the need to manage cluster infrastructure; Databricks handles compute provisioning automatically
options.C: Serverless warehouses require a dedicated VPC
options.D: Serverless warehouses do not support Delta Lake tables

questions.explanation: Serverless SQL warehouses are fully managed by Databricks — no VPC configuration, faster startup than classic warehouses, and automatic scaling. They are best for reducing operational overhead. ## Domain 5: Data Governance

questions.text: Which of the following commands grants the necessary permission for a group called "team" to view existing tables in a database called "customers"?

options.A: GRANT VIEW ON CATALOG customers TO team;
options.B: GRANT CREATE ON DATABASE customers TO team;
options.C: GRANT USAGE ON CATALOG team TO customers;
options.D: GRANT CREATE ON DATABASE team TO customers;
options.E: GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE customers TO team;

questions.explanation: GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE/SCHEMA allows the grantee to see (navigate/list) the database and its tables without granting SELECT or write access.

questions.text: Which of the following commands grants full privileges on the "customers" database to a "team" group?

options.A: GRANT USAGE ON DATABASE customers TO team;
options.B: GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE team TO customers;
options.C: GRANT SELECT PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE customers TO teams;
options.D: GRANT SELECT CREATE MODIFY USAGE PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE customers TO team;
options.E: GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE customers TO team;

questions.explanation: GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON object TO principal grants all applicable privileges (SELECT, MODIFY, CREATE, USAGE, etc.) on the specified object.

questions.text: At which level does Unity Catalog manage permissions in the Databricks platform?

options.A: Workspace level
options.B: Account level (across workspaces)
options.C: Cloud storage level
options.D: Data plane level

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog operates at the Databricks account level, providing a single governance layer that spans multiple workspaces within the same account.

questions.text: Which section of the Databricks UI is used to manage table permissions and grants?

options.A: User Settings
options.B: Admin UI
options.C: Workspace admin settings
options.D: User access control lists

questions.explanation: The Data Explorer (now Catalog Explorer in newer versions) provides a UI for browsing catalog, schema, and table objects and managing permissions via a graphical interface.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a privilege available in Unity Catalog?

options.A: SELECT
options.B: MODIFY
options.C: DELETE
options.D: CREATE TABLE

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog privileges include SELECT, MODIFY (which covers updates and deletes on data), CREATE, USAGE, EXECUTE (for functions), READ VOLUME, WRITE VOLUME, etc. DELETE is not a separate privilege — data deletion is covered by MODIFY.

questions.text: Which SQL command transfers ownership of a table to a group?

options.A: ALTER TABLE table_name OWNER TO 'group_name'
options.B: TRANSFER OWNER table_name TO 'group_name'
options.C: GRANT OWNER table_name TO 'group_name'
options.D: ALTER OWNER ON table_name TO 'group_name'

questions.explanation: In Databricks, table ownership is transferred using ALTER TABLE table_name OWNER TO principal (or ALTER TABLE ... SET OWNER TO ...).

questions.text: A data engineer needs to mark a table as containing PII (Personally Identifiable Information) data. Which of the following approaches is correct in Unity Catalog?

options.A: Apply a table tag or comment indicating PII classification using ALTER TABLE ... SET TBLPROPERTIES
options.B: Store PII tables in a separate DBFS location
options.C: Create a separate catalog named "PII" and move all such tables there
options.D: Grant restricted permissions to all users on the table

questions.explanation: In Unity Catalog (and Databricks SQL), you can use table tags (ALTER TABLE ... SET TAGS) or TBLPROPERTIES to add metadata like PII classification. Row-level security and column masking can then be applied on top.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the three-level namespace used by Unity Catalog?

options.A: workspace.database.table
options.B: catalog.schema.table
options.C: metastore.catalog.table
options.D: account.workspace.table

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog uses a three-level namespace: catalog.schema.table (e.g., main.sales.orders). This replaces the two-level hive metastore namespace (database.table).

questions.text: Which of the following is a true statement about the Unity Catalog metastore?

options.A: Each Databricks workspace has its own metastore
options.B: There is exactly one metastore per Databricks account globally
options.C: There can be one metastore per region within a Databricks account
options.D: Metastores are created automatically for each catalog

questions.explanation: You can have one Unity Catalog metastore per Databricks account per cloud region. Multiple workspaces within the same region can share a single metastore.

questions.text: Which feature of Unity Catalog enables tracking how data flows from source tables through transformations to downstream tables?

options.A: Audit logs
options.B: Data lineage
options.C: Delta Sharing
options.D: Row-level security

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog automatically captures data lineage for tables and views, showing upstream and downstream dependencies so data engineers can understand data flow and impact of changes.

questions.text: Which Unity Catalog feature enables sharing data with external organizations or across different cloud platforms without copying the data?

options.A: Data lineage
options.B: Row-level security
options.C: Delta Sharing
options.D: Audit logs

questions.explanation: Delta Sharing is an open protocol for sharing live data securely with other organizations, even if they use different cloud providers or don't have Databricks. It is integrated with Unity Catalog.

questions.text: How are audit logs stored in Unity Catalog when configured for compliance purposes?

options.A: Stored in the Unity Catalog metastore database tables
options.B: Stored as JSON files in a cloud storage location configured in the account console
options.C: Stored automatically in Delta tables in each workspace
options.D: Stored only in the Databricks control plane and not accessible to customers

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog audit logs capture all data access events and are delivered as JSON files to a cloud storage location (S3, ADLS, GCS) configured by the account admin.

questions.text: A new data engineering team has been assigned to work on a project and needs access to an existing database. Which Unity Catalog object should be granted to the team so they can access data using physical objects like tables, while sharing across teams?

options.A: Grant access on the workspace
options.B: Share the notebook with the team
options.C: Grant the team USAGE on the database (schema) and SELECT on the tables
options.D: Create a separate Unity Catalog metastore for the team

questions.explanation: In Unity Catalog, to share data between teams, you grant USAGE on the parent containers (catalog, schema) and SELECT (or other data-access privileges) on the specific tables. This is the multi-level permission model.

questions.text: In Unity Catalog, which of the following SQL commands correctly describes how to retrieve the path/location of a database?

options.A: SHOW DATABASE customer360
options.B: DESCRIBE DATABASE EXTENDED customer360
options.C: LIST DATABASE customer360
options.D: SHOW LOCATION customer360

questions.explanation: DESCRIBE DATABASE EXTENDED (or DESCRIBE SCHEMA EXTENDED) returns metadata about the database including its storage location/path, owner, and any comments.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the behavior of GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES in Unity Catalog?

options.A: Grants only SELECT and MODIFY on all tables
options.B: Grants all applicable privileges on the specified object to the specified principal
options.C: Grants all privileges across the entire metastore
options.D: Only applicable to catalog-level objects

questions.explanation: GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON object TO principal grants every privilege that is applicable to that specific object type (e.g., for a table: SELECT, MODIFY, CREATE, etc.).

questions.text: In the context of Unity Catalog, which of the following roles has the highest level of administrative authority?

options.A: Workspace admin
options.B: Catalog owner
options.C: Metastore admin
options.D: Schema owner

questions.explanation: The metastore admin has the highest authority in Unity Catalog — they can manage all catalogs, schemas, tables, users, and groups within the metastore. Workspace admins manage workspace-level settings but have more limited data governance scope. ## Section 6: Databricks Asset Bundles & CI/CD

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of Databricks Asset Bundles (DAB)?

options.A: To provide a visual UI for creating Databricks jobs and pipelines
options.B: To sync Git repositories directly into the Databricks workspace for notebook development
options.C: To define and deploy Databricks projects — including code, jobs, pipelines, and configurations — as versioned YAML files enabling CI/CD workflows
options.D: To replace Delta Live Tables with a YAML-based streaming framework

questions.explanation: DAB (now called Declarative Automation Bundles) is an infrastructure-as-code tool. It packages source files, job/pipeline definitions, and workspace configurations into a YAML-based project structure deployed via the Databricks CLI. It enables source control, code review, testing, and CI/CD — not UI-based development (A), not just notebook sync (B), and it complements rather than replaces DLT (D).

questions.text: Which of the following best describes what a Databricks Asset Bundle contains?

options.A: Only Python notebooks and SQL scripts
options.B: Only cluster configurations and compute settings
options.C: Source files, resource definitions (jobs, pipelines), infrastructure configs, unit/integration tests, and environment target settings
options.D: Only the databricks.yml configuration file

questions.explanation: A bundle is a complete project unit containing: cloud infrastructure configurations, source files (notebooks, scripts), resource definitions (jobs, pipelines, dashboards, MLflow components), unit and integration tests, and deployment targets. It is not limited to just notebooks (A), just clusters (B), or just a YAML file (D).

questions.text: A data engineer wants to adopt CI/CD practices for deploying Databricks jobs across development, staging, and production workspaces. Which Databricks feature is the recommended approach?

options.A: Databricks Repos with Git branching
options.B: Manual job creation via the Databricks UI in each workspace
options.C: Databricks Asset Bundles with the Databricks CLI
options.D: Terraform with the Databricks provider only

questions.explanation: Databricks officially recommends Asset Bundles (Declarative Automation Bundles) as the primary CI/CD approach. They natively support multiple deployment targets, YAML-defined resources, and integrate with GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, and other CI/CD platforms.
